entries.title: Restraint and Seclusion
entries.summary: This policy outlines when physical restraint and seclusion are permitted to be used in Victorian government schools and lists practices that are not permitted to be used in Victorian government schools. This policy details reporting and notification requirements and the responsibilities school and department staff have in relation to incidents of physical restraint and seclusion.

chapters.title: Critical information about restraint and seclusion — restraint, seclusion, physical, prevent, expected, behaviour
When physical restraint and seclusion are permitted — physical, restraint, physically, being, restrained, seclusion
When physical restraint and seclusion are prohibited — DOCX, COVID, physical, restraint, seclusion, prohibited

Immediate response to a physical restraint or seclusion incident — incident, emergency, physical, restraint, seclusion, immediate
Reporting — 3 hours, safety act 2004, ISOC, EWSS, incident, incidents
Provide support — EWSS, wellbeing, physical, restraint, seclusion, employee
Document the incident — incident, physical, restraint, seclusion, records, document

Definitions for restraint and seclusion — BSP, BCBA, RMP, behaviour, restraint, physical

entries.title: School Council – Composition, Eligibility and Office Bearers
entries.summary: From the 2026 school year, school council members, except VIT registered teachers and students, are required to hold a Working with Children Clearance (WWCC) (either a volunteer WWCC, or employee WWCC if they already have this), when nominating for a role on school council. Current (as at commencement of Term 1 2026)  school council members are not required to obtain a WWCC until they nominate for re-election/co-option.
